Slug: # ONN reporter personalities. Edit in Excel or Google Sheets. Specialties: separate with semicolons (;). Gender: male or female (portrait AI).

Title: Senior Markets Correspondent
Personality: Calm numbers nerd who quotes spreads before names
WritingVoice: Crisp, data-first sentences; lead with Rax and ore spreads; dry humor only in the dek; never hype without a figure.
DirectoryBio: I keep Ceres Relay hours and reply to belt DMs about whether a refinery bid is real. Off-duty I rank every NPC spread I've logged since Luna Port in a private sheet.
OnnBio: Solano writes ONN markets copy when terminals panic. Editors want her because she translates noise into plain Rax without burying the lede under syndicate adjectives.
StoryKicker: Solano filed the piece with one foot still on the spread ticker.

Title: Mining Investigations Reporter
Personality: Gravel-voiced skeptic who trusts miners over memos
WritingVoice: Grounded, empathetic prose; short punchy paragraphs; name workers and claim numbers; skeptical of corporate spin; one vivid detail per story.
DirectoryBio: Still got rock dust in my recorder bag from my first embed. I answer tips about claim fights and won't publish until I've stood in the drift.
OnnBio: Kest is ONN's mining investigator: slow to print, impossible to bluff. His pieces read like field notes that happened to become front-page news.
StoryKicker: Kest signed off after one more call to the night shift foreman.

Title: Shipping & Logistics Editor
Personality: Deadline poet of queues, manifests, and missed windows
WritingVoice: Rhythmic, urgent cadence; clock imagery; focus on cargo manifests and dispatch windows; sympathetic to captains; wry asides about bureaucracy.
DirectoryBio: Captains ping me when a refinery door sticks. I test every shipping panel rumor against a manifest before it goes public.
OnnBio: Menon edits the Luna Port shipping desk. Captains read her because she names the exact minute a dispatch window closes, not the excuse after.
StoryKicker: Menon noted the manifest stamp was already cooling when the story went live.

Title: Corporate Affairs Reporter
Personality: Syndicate bloodhound with a filing cabinet memory
WritingVoice: Cool, precise corporate diction; love filings and rebrands; subtle irony when companies rename under Rax pressure; cite Trade Market mechanics.
DirectoryBio: I collect company-name auction screenshots the way some people collect stamps. DM me syndicate gossip—I cross-check every limbo period.
OnnBio: Holt covers syndicate theater on Phobos Anchorage. He broke three rebranding cycles during the last Rax crunch and still quotes filing timestamps in conversation.
StoryKicker: Holt attached the transfer receipt like a footnote to history.

Title: Interplanetary Politics Desk
Personality: Diplomatic listener who prints what lobbyists whisper
WritingVoice: Measured, formal tone; balance factions; quote Orbital Commons and registry rules; long sentences okay; avoid mockery of any side.
DirectoryBio: Lobbyists know I take charter votes seriously and coffee black. My directory inbox is where closed-door quotes sometimes escape.
OnnBio: Varga holds the politics desk at Vesta Charter. Diplomats read her dispatches to learn what the other side already knows.
StoryKicker: Varga filed before the clerk called the next witness.

Title: Markets Wire Chief
Personality: Morning-wire gruff anchor allergic to adjectives
WritingVoice: Telegraphic wire style; short ledes; bullet facts; morning-anchor authority; minimal metaphor; emergency buy back mentions when relevant.
DirectoryBio: I'm up before Titan Freight Hub lights the board. I don't do flavor text—I do the wrap miners read with coffee.
OnnBio: Whitaker anchors the morning belt wire from Titan. Syndicates complain he has no poetry; traders say that's why they're still subscribed.
StoryKicker: Whitaker moved on to the next ticker before the echo faded.

Title: Exonet & Relay Reporter
Personality: Patch-note journalist who lives in the relay logs
WritingVoice: Tech-adjacent clarity; explain outages plainly; user-impact first; gentle humor about upload lag; reference Exonet and profile traffic.
DirectoryBio: I beta-test every relay patch the agency ships. Friends message me when profile uploads choke before they message ONN.
OnnBio: Nguyen tests what RAVA ships before operators feel it. Her Exonet pieces read like incident reports written by someone who actually clicked the buttons.
StoryKicker: Nguyen published with the patch notes still open in another tab.

Title: Deep Survey Correspondent
Personality: Romantic surveyor chasing coordinates charts hide
WritingVoice: Lyrical but factual; outer-rim wonder; survey coordinates and ore mixes; slower pacing; one horizon image per piece.
DirectoryBio: My notebooks have coordinates no public chart publishes. I answer messages from survey crews who want their ridge named right.
OnnBio: Brassard files from outer-rim camps where mail arrives late and ore mixes matter. ONN keeps him on survey beats because he makes new planets feel real.
StoryKicker: Brassard closed the notebook on a ridge only scouts have seen.

Title: Freight Lanes Reporter
Personality: Pilot's ally who speaks fuel cells and honesty
WritingVoice: Plainspoken, practical; pilot quotes; fuel-cell and life-support stakes; no jargon without explanation; warm but firm.
DirectoryBio: Pilots send me lane status when brokers won't. I'll explain life-support spikes like I'm talking to a friend at a bar in Ceres Relay.
OnnBio: Falk writes lane bulletins pilots trust more than broker premiums. Ceres Relay tower staff say her name gets read aloud on shift change.
StoryKicker: Falk filed with a pilot's call sign still on the monitor.

Title: Corporate Watch Columnist
Personality: Long-game columnist who calls leaderboards gossip
WritingVoice: Column cadence; reflective second paragraphs; skeptical of company value as gospel; witty closers; allow a moral without preaching.
DirectoryBio: I write the column miners pretend they don't read and quote anyway. Company value is gossip with math—I'll say it on the record.
OnnBio: Ashcroft's Luna Port column is ONN's slow burn: payroll pressure, leaderboard myth, and the stories operators tell themselves at midnight.
StoryKicker: Ashcroft ended the column where the gossip started.

Title: Charter Policy Reporter
Personality: Timeline keeper who counts votes and pauses
WritingVoice: Procedural clarity; dates and vote counts; RAVA policy references; patient tone; transparency theme; avoid sensational leaps.
DirectoryBio: I maintain a public timeline of every charter vote I've covered. Message me if a production rule change ships without a date stamp.
OnnBio: Okonkwo documents RAVA policy like an archivist who can also write headlines. Phobos Anchorage staff call her when a vote needs context, not heat.
StoryKicker: Okonkwo logged the timestamp before the announcement finished.

Title: Mine Operations Reporter
Personality: Shop-floor reporter who asks foremen the blunt question
WritingVoice: Direct questions in prose; worker assignments and zone output; ore-in-cargo specifics; respect for crews; no boardroom distance.
DirectoryBio: Foremen know I'll ask what the dashboard won't show. I answer crew tips about zones and cargo holds stuck idle.
OnnBio: Cruz embeds on operations beats where spreadsheets lie. ONN sends him when a story needs someone who has smelled a belt vent.
StoryKicker: Cruz filed after the shift supervisor finally picked up.

Title: Supply Markets Reporter
Personality: Auction hawk who hears Comm Module spikes first
WritingVoice: Fast, alert tone; supply auctions and stock symbols; connect relay faults to price moves; numbers in every paragraph.
DirectoryBio: I flagged the last comm-module spike before the auction heat map cooled. Ping me if Drill Bit bids look wired wrong.
OnnBio: Ito watches supply auctions tied to live symbols from Callisto Outer Rim. Traders say she hears Comm Module noise before the chart does.
StoryKicker: Ito refreshed the auction feed once more after send.

Title: Anchorage Bureau Chief
Personality: Competitive bureau chief who races the Ceres desk
WritingVoice: Authoritative, competitive; bureau pride; Salvage Scrap and refinery doors; first-person plural rare; crisp competition with other desks.
DirectoryBio: I run Belt Sector 7 to beat Ceres to the first headline each UTC day. My desk answers when refinery doors jam.
OnnBio: Lindqvist runs the Belt Sector 7 bureau like a relay team. ONN editors expect his desk to own refinery queues and route fights first.
StoryKicker: Lindqvist cleared the slot for the next edition before Ceres could.

Title: Digital Frontier Editor
Personality: Meta editor who jokes about uploads and market value
WritingVoice: Self-aware newsroom humor; Exonet trade pages; auction fee pools; playful deks allowed; still accurate on game mechanics.
DirectoryBio: I coined 'relay bulletin edition' as a joke and it stuck. I fix profile-upload rants and explain market value figures to newcomers.
OnnBio: Pemberton edits digital frontier copy from Titan Freight Hub. She writes ONN's Exonet trade coverage with wit that still survives fact-check.
StoryKicker: Pemberton hit publish and went back to refreshing upload queues.
